page: Microsoft Azure D48s_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 48 MB of L3 cache and supports advanced vector extensions like AVX-512F and AVX-512 VNNI, alongside hardware-accelerated cryptography features such as AES and SHA_NI. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU.

This instance does not include any local storage or bundled GPUs, meaning all storage requirements must be met via external Premium Storage, which the instance is capable of supporting. Additionally, it does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The lack of local storage and GPU hardware represents a tradeoff, making it less suitable for workloads requiring high-speed local scratch disks or hardware-accelerated graphics, but optimizing it for workloads that rely on network-attached storage and standard CPU execution.

Benchmark results indicate strong performance in several areas, including a Passmark CPU score of 48423.66, a Passmark memory score of 2811.61, and strong database operations performance at 21546.46. Image processing and text generation for small LLM models also register in the strong tier. These characteristics make the D48s_v5 highly effective for database operations, enterprise application hosting, web serving, and moderate-scale text processing or software compilation tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure D48s_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DSv5 family, powered by a 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 48 dedicated vCPUs (24 physical cores) and 192.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 instance features AVX-512 vector extensions and SHA/AES cryptographic acceleration. It lacks local storage and GPU hardware, relying instead on its Premium Storage capability for external disk attachments. Benchmark data highlights strong performance in database operations, memory access, and image processing. The balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it qualitatively cost-efficient for enterprise applications, database hosting, and general-purpose workloads that do not require local scratch disks or GPU acceleration.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48s_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and Premium Storage capability, delivering strong database and memory benchmark performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48s_v5 is a general-purpose VM with 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and strong database and memory benchmark performance.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring forty-eight dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and premium storage capabilities for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated vCPUs and 192.0 GB of RAM providing a balanced 1:4 resource ratio
Intel Xeon 8370C processor with AVX-512 and cryptographic hardware acceleration
Strong benchmark performance in database operations and memory access
Premium Storage capable with no bundled local storage or GPU hardware
Suited for enterprise applications, database hosting, and web serving
